expert.name: Peter Attia
expert.bio: Physician focused on the applied science of longevity. Former surgical oncologist at Johns Hopkins. Host of The Drive podcast. Author of Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity. Emphasizes preventing the 'Four Horsemen' of chronic disease.

supplements.name: EPA/DHA (Fish Oil)
supplements.description: High-quality omega-3 fatty acids for cardiovascular and brain health

supplements.name: Vitamin D3
supplements.description: Essential for immune function, bone health, and metabolic regulation

supplements.name: Magnesium (various forms)
supplements.description: Critical mineral for 300+ enzymatic reactions. Most people deficient.

supplements.name: Creatine Monohydrate
supplements.description: Performance and cognitive benefits with excellent safety profile

supplements.name: Methylfolate + B12
supplements.description: Essential for methylation and homocysteine metabolism

supplements.name: Baby Aspirin
supplements.description: Cardiovascular protection for those with elevated risk

protocols.name: Zone 2 Training Protocol
protocols.description: Foundational aerobic training at lactate threshold 1 (LT1). The highest intensity where lactate remains stable ~2mmol/L. Should be able to maintain nasal breathing or conversation.

details.progression: Add 10% duration every 2 weeks until 4+ hours/week total
details.testing: Lactate meter preferred, MAF test alternative

protocols.rationale: Builds mitochondrial density, improves fat oxidation, enhances metabolic flexibility. Foundation for all other training.

protocols.name: VO2 Max Training
protocols.description: High-intensity intervals to improve maximum oxygen uptake. Critical for longevity - strong correlation with all-cause mortality.
protocols.rationale: VO2 max is one of strongest predictors of longevity. Moving from bottom 25% to top 25% associated with 5x reduction in mortality.

protocols.name: Stability Training
protocols.description: Foundation for all movement. DNS-based approach to rebuild proper motor patterns and prevent injury.
protocols.rationale: Prevents injury that derails training. Enables heavy strength work safely. Often neglected but critical.

protocols.name: Strength Training - Centenarian Decathlon
protocols.description: Train for the activities you want to do in your 90s. Grip strength, carrying grandchildren, getting off floor, climbing stairs.

details.grip_work: Farmer carries, dead hangs, plate pinches
details.eccentric_emphasis: Critical for injury prevention and muscle quality

protocols.rationale: Muscle mass peaks at 25, then declines. Must actively maintain to preserve function into old age.

protocols.name: Four Horsemen Prevention Protocol
protocols.description: Comprehensive approach to preventing cardiovascular disease, cancer, neurodegenerative disease, and metabolic disease.

protocols.name: ApoB Management Protocol
protocols.description: Primary focus on apoB as the causal driver of atherosclerosis. More important than LDL-C.

details.target: <60 mg/dL for aggressive prevention, <80 mg/dL standard

protocols.rationale: apoB is the causal particle in atherosclerosis. Each apoB particle can deposit cholesterol in arterial wall. Lower is better with no lower limit identified.

protocols.name: CGM Protocol for Metabolic Health
protocols.description: Use continuous glucose monitoring to optimize metabolic health and understand individual responses.
protocols.frequency: 1-2 weeks every quarter, or continuous if pre-diabetic

details.learnings: Identify personal glucose responses to foods, meal timing, exercise effects
details.exercise_timing: Post-meal walks, Zone 2 fasted or fed comparison

protocols.name: Sleep Optimization Protocol
protocols.description: Sleep is the foundation. Poor sleep undermines every other intervention.

interventions.name: Sauna Protocol
interventions.description: Regular sauna use associated with reduced all-cause mortality in Finnish studies
interventions.rationale: Heat shock proteins, growth hormone release, cardiovascular conditioning, possibly dementia risk reduction

interventions.name: Cold Exposure
interventions.description: Cold plunge or cold shower for metabolic and mood benefits
interventions.timing: Not immediately post-strength training (blunts hypertrophy signal)
interventions.rationale: Dopamine release, brown fat activation, mental resilience, inflammation reduction

interventions.name: Continuous Glucose Monitor
interventions.description: Real-time feedback on glucose for metabolic optimization
interventions.rationale: Identify personal glycemic responses, optimize meal composition and timing

nutrition_principles.guidance: Protein is the most important macronutrient to get right. Target 1g per pound of lean body mass minimum for muscle preservation.
nutrition_principles.details: Distribute evenly across meals (40-50g per meal). Leucine threshold matters. Animal sources superior for muscle protein synthesis.

nutrition_principles.guidance: Carb needs highly individual. Athletes need more. Sedentary need less. CGM can personalize.
nutrition_principles.details: Prioritize around training. Focus on whole food sources. Avoid liquid calories and refined sugars.

nutrition_principles.guidance: Eating window of 10-12 hours. Not extreme fasting. Focus on not eating late at night.
nutrition_principles.details: Last meal 3+ hours before bed. Protein early in day. Not about calorie restriction.

nutrition_principles.guidance: No safe level for optimal health. If drinking, minimize. Red wine not protective.
nutrition_principles.details: Disrupts sleep architecture. Increases cancer risk. Impacts metabolic health. Social benefits real but weigh against health costs.
